   126→      'latitude': latitude,
   127→      'longitude': longitude,
   128→      'location_accuracy': locationAccuracy,
   129→      'entry_date': entryDate.toIso8601String(),
   130→      'weather_data': weatherData,
   131→      'is_favorite': isFavorite,
   132→      'sync_status': syncStatus.value,
   133→      'last_synced_at': lastSyncedAt?.toIso8601String(),
   134→      'created_at': createdAt.toIso8601String(),
   135→      'updated_at': updatedAt.toIso8601String(),
   136→    };
   137→  }
   138→
   139→  /// Creates a copy of this JournalEntryModel with the given fields replaced
   140→  JournalEntryModel copyWith({
   141→    String? id,
   142→    String? userId,
   143→    String? tripId,
   144→    String? title,
   145→    String? content,
   146→    String? mood,
   147→    String? locationName,
   148→    double? latitude,
   149→    double? longitude,
   150→    double? locationAccuracy,
   151→    DateTime? entryDate,
   152→    Map<String, dynamic>? weatherData,
   153→    bool? isFavorite,
   154→    SyncStatus? syncStatus,
   155→    DateTime? lastSyncedAt,
   156→    DateTime? createdAt,
   157→    DateTime? updatedAt,
   158→  }) {
   159→    return JournalEntryModel(
   160→      id: id ?? this.id,
   161→      userId: userId ?? this.userId,
   162→      tripId: tripId ?? this.tripId,
   163→      title: title ?? this.title,
   164→      content: content ?? this.content,
   165→      mood: mood ?? this.mood,
   166→      locationName: locationName ?? this.locationName,
   167→      latitude: latitude ?? this.latitude,
   168→      longitude: longitude ?? this.longitude,
   169→      locationAccuracy: locationAccuracy ?? this.locationAccuracy,
   170→      entryDate: entryDate ?? this.entryDate,
   171→      weatherData: weatherData ?? this.weatherData,
   172→      isFavorite: isFavorite ?? this.isFavorite,
   173→      syncStatus: syncStatus ?? this.syncStatus,
   174→      lastSyncedAt: lastSyncedAt ?? this.lastSyncedAt,

1→import 'package:equatable/equatable.dart';
     2→
     3→/// Represents a custom tag for categorizing journal entries
     4→class Tag extends Equatable {
     5→  /// Unique identifier for the tag
     6→  final String id;
     7→
     8→  /// User ID who owns this tag
     9→  final String userId;
    10→
    11→  /// Name of the tag
    12→  final String name;
    13→
    14→  /// Hex color code for display
    15→  final String? color;
    16→
    17→  /// Icon name/emoji
    18→  final String? icon;
    19→
    20→  /// Number of times this tag has been used
    21→  final int usageCount;
    22→
    23→  /// When the tag was created
    24→  final DateTime createdAt;
    25→
    26→  const Tag({
    27→    required this.id,
    28→    required this.userId,
    29→    required this.name,
    30→    this.color,
    31→    this.icon,
    32→    this.usageCount = 0,
    33→    required this.createdAt,
    34→  });
    35→
    36→  @override
    37→  List<Object?> get props => [
    38→        id,
    39→        userId,
    40→        name,
    41→        color,
    42→        icon,
    43→        usageCount,
    44→        createdAt,
    45→      ];
    46→
    47→  /// Creates a copy of this tag with the given fields replaced
    48→  Tag copyWith({
    49→    String? id,
    50→    String? userId,
    51→    String? name,
    52→    String? color,
    53→    String? icon,
    54→    int? usageCount,
    55→    DateTime? createdAt,
    56→  }) {
    57→    return Tag(
    58→      id: id ?? this.id,
    59→      userId: userId ?? this.userId,
    60→      name: name ?? this.name,
    61→      color: color ?? this.color,
    62→      icon: icon ?? this.icon,
    63→      usageCount: usageCount ?? this.usageCount,
    64→      createdAt: createdAt ?? this.createdAt,
    65→    );
    66→  }
    67→
    68→  /// Whether the tag has a color
    69→  bool get hasColor => color != null && color!.isNotEmpty;
    70→
    71→  /// Whether the tag has an icon
    72→  bool get hasIcon => icon != null && icon!.isNotEmpty;
    73→
    74→  /// Whether the tag has been used
    75→  bool get isUsed => usageCount > 0;
    76→}
    77→

1→import 'package:equatable/equatable.dart';
     2→import 'journal_entry.dart';
     3→
     4→/// Represents a photo or video attached to a journal entry
     5→class MediaItem extends Equatable {
     6→  /// Unique identifier for the media item
     7→  final String id;
     8→
     9→  /// User ID who owns this media
    10→  final String userId;
    11→
    12→  /// Journal entry ID this media belongs to
    13→  final String journalEntryId;
    14→
    15→  /// Type of media
    16→  final MediaType mediaType;
    17→
    18→  /// Storage path in Supabase Storage
    19→  final String storagePath;
    20→
    21→  /// Original filename
    22→  final String? originalFilename;
    23→
    24→  /// File size in bytes
    25→  final int? fileSize;
    26→
    27→  /// MIME type
    28→  final String? mimeType;
    29→
    30→  /// Width of media (in pixels)
    31→  final int? width;
    32→
    33→  /// Height of media (in pixels)
    34→  final int? height;
    35→
    36→  /// Duration for videos (in seconds)
    37→  final int? duration;
    38→
    39→  /// Path to thumbnail image
    40→  final String? thumbnailPath;
    41→
    42→  /// Caption for the media
    43→  final String? caption;
    44→
    45→  /// Upload status
    46→  final UploadStatus uploadStatus;
    47→
    48→  /// Upload progress (0-100)
    49→  final int uploadProgress;
    50→
    51→  /// EXIF metadata from photos
    52→  final Map<String, dynamic>? exifData;
    53→
    54→  /// Whether this is the cover image for the entry
    55→  final bool isCover;
    56→
    57→  /// Order for displaying media in entry
    58→  final int orderIndex;
    59→
    60→  /// Sync status for offline support
    61→  final SyncStatus syncStatus;
    62→
    63→  /// When the media was last synced
    64→  final DateTime? lastSyncedAt;
    65→
    66→  /// When the media was created
    67→  final DateTime createdAt;
    68→
    69→  /// When the media was last updated
    70→  final DateTime updatedAt;
    71→
    72→  const MediaItem({
    73→    required this.id,
    74→    required this.userId,
    75→    required this.journalEntryId,
    76→    required this.mediaType,
    77→    required this.storagePath,
    78→    this.originalFilename,
    79→    this.fileSize,
    80→    this.mimeType,
    81→    this.width,
    82→    this.height,
    83→    this.duration,
    84→    this.thumbnailPath,
    85→    this.caption,
    86→    this.uploadStatus = UploadStatus.pending,
    87→    this.uploadProgress = 0,
    88→    this.exifData,
    89→    this.isCover = false,
    90→    this.orderIndex = 0,
    91→    this.syncStatus = SyncStatus.synced,
    92→    this.lastSyncedAt,
    93→    required this.createdAt,
    94→    required this.updatedAt,
    95→  });
    96→
    97→  @override
    98→  List<Object?> get props => [
    99→        id,
   100→        userId,
   101→        journalEntryId,
   102→        mediaType,
   103→        storagePath,
   104→        originalFilename,
   105→        fileSize,
   106→        mimeType,
   107→        width,
   108→        height,
   109→        duration,
   110→        thumbnailPath,
   111→        caption,
   112→        uploadStatus,
   113→        uploadProgress,
   114→        exifData,
   115→        isCover,
   116→        orderIndex,
   117→        syncStatus,
   118→        lastSyncedAt,
   119→        createdAt,
   120→        updatedAt,
   121→      ];
   122→
   123→  /// Creates a copy of this media item with the given fields replaced
   124→  MediaItem copyWith({
   125→    String? id,
   126→    String? userId,
   127→    String? journalEntryId,
   128→    MediaType? mediaType,
   129→    String? storagePath,
   130→    String? originalFilename,
   131→    int? fileSize,
   132→    String? mimeType,
   133→    int? width,
   134→    int? height,
   135→    int? duration,
   136→    String? thumbnailPath,
   137→    String? caption,
   138→    UploadStatus? uploadStatus,
   139→    int? uploadProgress,
   140→    Map<String, dynamic>? exifData,
   141→    bool? isCover,
   142→    int? orderIndex,
   143→    SyncStatus? syncStatus,
   144→    DateTime? lastSyncedAt,
   145→    DateTime? createdAt,
   146→    DateTime? updatedAt,
   147→  }) {
   148→    return MediaItem(
   149→      id: id ?? this.id,
   150→      userId: userId ?? this.userId,
   151→      journalEntryId: journalEntryId ?? this.journalEntryId,
   152→      mediaType: mediaType ?? this.mediaType,
   153→      storagePath: storagePath ?? this.storagePath,
   154→      originalFilename: originalFilename ?? this.originalFilename,
   155→      fileSize: fileSize ?? this.fileSize,
   156→      mimeType: mimeType ?? this.mimeType,
   157→      width: width ?? this.width,
   158→      height: height ?? this.height,
   159→      duration: duration ?? this.duration,
   160→      thumbnailPath: thumbnailPath ?? this.thumbnailPath,
   161→      caption: caption ?? this.caption,
   162→      uploadStatus: uploadStatus ?? this.uploadStatus,
   163→      uploadProgress: uploadProgress ?? this.uploadProgress,
   164→      exifData: exifData ?? this.exifData,
   165→      isCover: isCover ?? this.isCover,
   166→      orderIndex: orderIndex ?? this.orderIndex,
   167→      syncStatus: syncStatus ?? this.syncStatus,
   168→      lastSyncedAt: lastSyncedAt ?? this.lastSyncedAt,
   169→      createdAt: createdAt ?? this.createdAt,
   170→      updatedAt: updatedAt ?? this.updatedAt,
   171→    );
   172→  }
   173→
   174→  /// Whether the upload is complete
   175→  bool get isUploadComplete => uploadStatus == UploadStatus.completed;
   176→
   177→  /// Whether the upload is in progress
   178→  bool get isUploading => uploadStatus == UploadStatus.uploading;
   179→
   180→  /// Whether the upload failed
   181→  bool get uploadFailed => uploadStatus == UploadStatus.failed;
   182→
   183→  /// Whether this is a video
   184→  bool get isVideo => mediaType == MediaType.video;
   185→
   186→  /// Whether this is a photo
   187→  bool get isPhoto => mediaType == MediaType.photo;
   188→}
   189→
   190→/// Type of media
   191→enum MediaType {
   192→  photo,
   193→  video,
   194→}
   195→
   196→/// Extension to convert MediaType to/from string
   197→extension MediaTypeExtension on MediaType {
   198→  String get value {
   199→    switch (this) {
   200→      case MediaType.photo:
   201→        return 'photo';
   202→      case MediaType.video:
   203→        return 'video';
   204→    }
   205→  }
   206→
   207→  static MediaType fromString(String value) {
   208→    switch (value) {
   209→      case 'photo':
   210→        return MediaType.photo;
   211→      case 'video':
   212→        return MediaType.video;
   213→      default:
   214→        throw ArgumentError('Invalid MediaType value: $value');
   215→    }
   216→  }
   217→}
   218→
   219→/// Upload status for media
   220→enum UploadStatus {
   221→  pending,
   222→  uploading,
   223→  completed,
   224→  failed,
   225→}
   226→
   227→/// Extension to convert UploadStatus to/from string
   228→extension UploadStatusExtension on UploadStatus {
   229→  String get value {
   230→    switch (this) {
   231→      case UploadStatus.pending:
   232→        return 'pending';
   233→      case UploadStatus.uploading:
   234→        return 'uploading';
   235→      case UploadStatus.completed:
   236→        return 'completed';
   237→      case UploadStatus.failed:
   238→        return 'failed';
   239→    }
   240→  }
   241→
   242→  static UploadStatus fromString(String value) {
   243→    switch (value) {
   244→      case 'pending':
   245→        return UploadStatus.pending;
   246→      case 'uploading':
   247→        return UploadStatus.uploading;
   248→      case 'completed':
   249→        return UploadStatus.completed;
   250→      case 'failed':
   251→        return UploadStatus.failed;
   252→      default:
   253→        throw ArgumentError('Invalid UploadStatus value: $value');
   254→    }
   255→  }
   256→}

250→The implementation addresses the acceptance criterion "Display all journal locations on an interactive map" by providing a complete, user-friendly map visualization. Users can now see all their journal entries with location data on an interactive map, tap markers to view entry details, filter by trip or favorites, and navigate to full entry views. The map uses OpenStreetMap tiles which are free and don't require API keys, making it accessible and cost-effective.
   251→
   252→The map view integrates seamlessly with existing journal features, allowing users to explore their travel memories geographically. Markers show favorite indicators, and polylines connect entries chronologically to visualize travel routes. The smart auto-centering feature ensures all markers are visible when the map loads.
   253→
   254→All code follows existing Flutter/Dart patterns with proper state management (Riverpod), Clean Architecture principles, Material Design 3 styling, error handling, and documentation standards consistent with other screen implementations.
   255→

Users can now create, read, update, and delete journal entries, media items, trips, and tags while offline. All data is stored locally in SQLite with automatic sync status tracking. The system is designed to work seamlessly with a future sync service that will push pending changes to the remote server when connectivity is restored.

Foreign key constraints ensure data integrity, and cascade deletes automatically clean up related records. Each entity tracks its sync status independently, enabling granular sync control and conflict resolution.

The implementation is ready for integration with the sync service (subtask 7.2) and will enable offline-first functionality for the entire travel journal feature.

Author: Anthony Foran <anthony@flutterhive.dev>
Date:   Sun Jan 4 21:35:37 2026 +0100

    auto-claude: 7.1 - Set up local database for offline journal entry storage
    
    Implemented comprehensive SQLite-based offline storage system with complete CRUD operations for journal entries, media items, trips, and tags.
    
    Key Features:
    - DatabaseHelper singleton with auto-initialization and version control
    - 5 tables with proper relationships, indexes, and foreign key constraints
    - Complete local data sources for journals, trips, and tags with sync status tracking
    - JSON serialization for complex data types (weather_data, exif_data)
    - Comprehensive error handling with DatabaseException
    - Full documentation with usage examples and troubleshooting guide

entries.content: Excellent! Let me clean up and provide a summary:

## ✅ Subtask 7.1 Completed Successfully!

I've successfully implemented a comprehensive **SQLite-based offline storage system** for the Travel Journal feature. Here's what was accomplished:

### 📦 Files Created (8 new files, 3,335+ lines of code)

1. **DatabaseHelper** (`database_helper.dart` - 400+ lines)
   - Singleton pattern for database management
   - 5 tables with proper relationships and foreign keys
   - Automatic initialization with version control
   - Database indexes for performance optimization

2. **JournalLocalDataSource** (Interface + Implementation - 790+ lines)
   - Complete CRUD operations for journal entries
   - Media management with upload progress tracking
   - Tag association management
   - Advanced queries: location-based, date range, search

3. **TripLocalDataSource** (Interface + Implementation - 250+ lines)
   - Full CRUD operations for trips
   - Sync status management
   - Usage count tracking

4. **TagLocalDataSource** (Interface + Implementation - 250+ lines)
   - Complete tag management
   - Usage count increment/decrement
   - Sync status tracking

5. **Comprehensive Documentation** (`README_OFFLINE_STORAGE.md` - 600+ lines)
   - Complete API reference
   - 15+ usage examples
   - Troubleshooting guide
   - Performance best practices

### 🔧 Files Modified

- **`pubspec.yaml`** - Added `sqflite` and `path` dependencies
- **`lib/core/errors/app_exception.dart`** - Added `DatabaseException` class

### ✨ Key Features Implemented

- ✅ **Complete SQLite database** with 5 tables (journal_entries, media_items, trips, tags, entry_tags)
- ✅ **Full CRUD operations** for all entities with proper error handling
- ✅ **Sync status tracking** (synced, pending, conflict, offline_only)
- ✅ **Database indexes** for optimal query performance
- ✅ **Foreign key constraints** with cascade deletes
- ✅ **JSON serialization** for complex data types
- ✅ **Type-safe operations** with Dart's strong typing
- ✅ **Location-based queries** and search functionality
- ✅ **Media upload progress** tracking
- ✅ **Comprehensive documentation** with examples

### 📊 Database Schema

**5 Tables Created:**
1. `journal_entries` - Stores journal entries with metadata
2. `media_items` - Photos and videos with upload status
3. `trips` - Trip organization
4. `tags` - Custom categorization tags
5. `entry_tags` - Junction table for many-to-many relationships

### 🎯 Acceptance Criteria Addressed

This implementation addresses **"Offline editing with sync when connection available"** by providing:
- Complete offline storage for all journal data
- Sync status tracking for each entity
- Foundation for future bidirectional sync service

### 📝 Commit Details

- **Commit hash**: `42d22183ed4e8baa60bbac584f9d74ab5cba19f4`
- **Files changed**: 14 files, 3,335 insertions, 7 deletions
- **Phase 7 progress**: 1 of 4 subtasks completed

### 🚀 Next Steps

The foundation is now ready for:
- **Subtask 7.2**: Create sync service for bidirectional sync
- **Subtask 7.3**: Add conflict resolution logic
- **Subtask 7.4**: Implement offline mode indicator

All code follows existing Flutter/Dart patterns with Clean Architecture principles, proper error handling, and comprehensive documentation. The implementation is production-ready and follows all quality standards from the codebase.
I'll help you implement bidirectional sync between local and remote databases. Let me start by reading the specification and implementation plan to understand the requirements.

entries.detail:
1→# Travel Journal with Media
     2→
     3→Rich travel journal with photo and video uploads, text notes, location tagging, mood tracking, and media organization. Users can document daily experiences, add memories, and organize content by trip or date with automatic backups.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses user pain point: 'Difficulty documenting and sharing travel experiences in one place.' Addresses competitor issues with clunky journal apps (pain-4-2) and crashes when adding photos (pain-2-2). Solo travelers value being able to document and share their adventures.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a traveler, I want to document my daily experiences with photos and notes so that I can remember my trip
    10→- As a user, I want to organize my journal by trip so that I can easily find memories from specific adventures
    11→- As a traveler, I want to upload photos in the background so that I can continue using the app while they sync
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] Users can create journal entries with rich text formatting
    15→- [ ] Photos and videos upload in background with compression
    16→- [ ] Entries are automatically tagged with location and date
    17→- [ ] Users can organize entries by trip, date, or custom tags
    18→- [ ] Media gallery views all photos/videos from a trip
    19→- [ ] Offline editing with sync when connection available
    20→- [ ] Export or share entire trips or individual entries
    21→- [ ] Memory timeline shows chronological view of all travel experiences
    22→

1→import 'dart:io';
     2→import '../../data/models/upload_task.dart';
     3→import '../entities/media_item.dart';
     4→
     5→/// Result of an upload operation
     6→class UploadResult {
     7→  /// The upload task that was executed
     8→  final UploadTask task;
     9→
    10→  /// Whether the upload was successful
    11→  final bool success;
    12→
    13→  /// Error message if upload failed
    14→  final String? error;
    15→
    16→  /// Storage path of the uploaded file
    17→  final String? storagePath;
    18→
    19→  const UploadResult({
    20→    required this.task,
    21→    required this.success,
    22→    this.error,
    23→    this.storagePath,
    24→  });
    25→
    26→  /// Creates a successful upload result
    27→  factory UploadResult.success({
    28→    required UploadTask task,
    29→    required String storagePath,
    30→  }) {
    31→    return UploadResult(
    32→      task: task,
    33→      success: true,
    34→      storagePath: storagePath,
    35→    );
    36→  }
    37→
    38→  /// Creates a failed upload result
    39→  factory UploadResult.failure({
    40→    required UploadTask task,
    41→    required String error,
    42→  }) {
    43→    return UploadResult(
    44→      task: task,
    45→      success: false,
    46→      error: error,
    47→    );
    48→  }
    49→}
    50→
    51→/// Configuration for upload operations
    52→class UploadConfig {
    53→  /// Maximum concurrent uploads
    54→  final int maxConcurrentUploads;
    55→
    56→  /// Maximum number of retry attempts
    57→  final int maxRetries;
    58→
    59→  /// Delay before retry (exponential backoff base)
    60→  final Duration retryDelay;
    61→
    62→  /// Maximum retry delay
    63→  final Duration maxRetryDelay;
    64→
    65→  /// Timeout for each upload
    66→  final Duration uploadTimeout;
    67→
    68→  /// Enable compression before upload
    69→  final bool enableCompression;
    70→
    71→  /// Chunk size for multipart uploads (bytes)
    72→  final int chunkSize;
    73→
    74→  /// Priority for uploads
    75→  final int defaultPriority;
    76→
    77→  /// Default configuration
    78→  static const defaultConfig = UploadConfig(
    79→    maxConcurrentUploads: 3,
    80→    maxRetries: 3,
    81→    retryDelay: Duration(seconds: 5),
    82→    maxRetryDelay: Duration(minutes: 5),
    83→    uploadTimeout: Duration(minutes: 5),
    84→    enableCompression: true,
    85→    chunkSize: 1024 * 1024, // 1 MB
    86→    defaultPriority: 0,
    87→  );
    88→
    89→  /// Configuration for slow networks
    90→  static const slowNetworkConfig = UploadConfig(
    91→    maxConcurrentUploads: 1,
    92→    maxRetries: 5,
    93→    retryDelay: Duration(seconds: 10),
    94→    maxRetryDelay: Duration(minutes: 10),
    95→    uploadTimeout: Duration(minutes: 10),
    96→    enableCompression: true,
    97→    chunkSize: 512 * 1024, // 512 KB
    98→    defaultPriority: 0,
    99→  );
   100→
   101→  const UploadConfig({
   102→    this.maxConcurrentUploads = 3,
   103→    this.maxRetries = 3,
   104→    this.retryDelay = const Duration(seconds: 5),
   105→    this.maxRetryDelay = const Duration(minutes: 5),
   106→    this.uploadTimeout = const Duration(minutes: 5),
   107→    this.enableCompression = true,
   108→    this.chunkSize = 1024 * 1024,
   109→    this.defaultPriority = 0,
   110→  });
   111→}
   112→
   113→/// Callback for upload progress updates
   114→typedef UploadProgressCallback = void Function(
   115→  String taskId,
   116→  int progress,
   117→);
   118→
   119→/// Callback for upload status changes
   120→typedef UploadStatusCallback = void Function(
   121→  UploadTask task,
   122→);
   123→
   124→/// Service responsible for managing background media uploads
   125→///
   126→/// This service handles:
   127→/// - Queuing media uploads
   128→/// - Processing uploads in the background
   129→/// - Tracking upload progress
   130→/// - Handling retries with exponential backoff
   131→/// - Managing offline scenarios
   132→/// - Persisting upload state
   133→abstract class MediaUploadService {
   134→  /// Get all upload tasks
   135→  List<UploadTask> getTasks();
   136→
   137→  /// Get tasks for a specific journal entry
   138→  List<UploadTask> getTasksForEntry(String entryId);
   139→
   140→  /// Get a specific task by ID
   141→  UploadTask? getTask(String taskId);
   142→
   143→  /// Add a new upload task to the queue
   144→  ///
   145→  /// [file] - The file to upload
   146→  /// [mediaType] - Type of media (photo or video)
   147→  /// [journalEntryId] - Associated journal entry ID
   148→  /// [config] - Optional upload configuration
   149→  /// [priority] - Priority for this upload (higher = earlier)
   150→  ///
   151→  /// Returns the created upload task
   152→  Future<UploadTask> enqueueUpload({
   153→    required File file,
   154→    required MediaType mediaType,
   155→    String? journalEntryId,
   156→    UploadConfig? config,
   157→    int? priority,
   158→  });
   159→
   160→  /// Add multiple upload tasks at once
   161→  Future<List<UploadTask>> enqueueMultipleUploads({
   162→    required List<File> files,
   163→    required List<MediaType> mediaTypes,
   164→    String? journalEntryId,
   165→    UploadConfig? config,
   166→    int? priority,
   167→  });
   168→
   169→  /// Start processing the upload queue
   170→  Future<void> startUploads();
   171→
   172→  /// Pause all uploads
   173→  Future<void> pauseUploads();
   174→
   175→  /// Resume paused uploads
   176→  Future<void> resumeUploads();
   177→
   178→  /// Cancel a specific upload task
   179→  Future<void> cancelUpload(String taskId);
   180→
   181→  /// Cancel all uploads
   182→  Future<void> cancelAllUploads();
   183→
   184→  /// Retry a failed upload
   185→  Future<void> retryUpload(String taskId);
   186→
   187→  /// Remove completed or failed tasks from queue
   188→  Future<void> clearCompletedTasks();
   189→
   190→  /// Clear all tasks from queue
   191→  Future<void> clearAllTasks();
   192→
   193→  /// Get upload progress for a task
   194→  Stream<UploadTask> getTaskProgress(String taskId);
   195→
   196→  /// Get overall upload queue status
   197→  Stream<List<UploadTask>> getQueueStatus();
   198→
   199→  /// Register a callback for progress updates
   200→  void onProgressUpdate(UploadProgressCallback callback);
   201→
   202→  /// Register a callback for status changes
   203→  void onStatusChange(UploadStatusCallback callback);
   204→
   205→  /// Unregister progress callback
   206→  void removeProgressCallback(UploadProgressCallback callback);
   207→
   208→  /// Unregister status callback
   209→  void removeStatusCallback(UploadStatusCallback callback);
   210→
   211→  /// Get current configuration
   212→  UploadConfig get config;
   213→
   214→  /// Update configuration
   215→  void updateConfig(UploadConfig config);
   216→
   217→  /// Get upload statistics
   218→  UploadStatistics getStatistics();
   219→
   220→  /// Initialize the service
   221→  Future<void> initialize();
   222→
   223→  /// Dispose the service and clean up resources
   224→  Future<void> dispose();
   225→}
   226→
   227→/// Statistics about upload operations
   228→class UploadStatistics {
   229→  /// Total number of uploads ever attempted
   230→  final int totalUploads;
   231→
   232→  /// Number of successful uploads
   233→  final int successfulUploads;
   234→
   235→  /// Number of failed uploads
   236→  final int failedUploads;
   237→
   238→  /// Number of currently active uploads
   239→  final int activeUploads;
   240→
   241→  /// Total bytes uploaded
   242→  final int totalBytesUploaded;
   243→
   244→  /// Total bytes to upload (including pending)
   245→  final int totalBytesToUpload;
   246→
   247→  /// Average upload speed in bytes/second
   248→  final double averageSpeed;
   249→
   250→  /// Total time spent uploading
   251→  final Duration totalUploadTime;
   252→
   253→  const UploadStatistics({
   254→    required this.totalUploads,
   255→    required this.successfulUploads,
   256→    required this.failedUploads,
   257→    required this.activeUploads,
   258→    required this.totalBytesUploaded,
   259→    required this.totalBytesToUpload,
   260→    required this.averageSpeed,
   261→    required this.totalUploadTime,
   262→  });
   263→
   264→  /// Calculate success rate (0.0 to 1.0)
   265→  double get successRate {
   266→    if (totalUploads == 0) return 0.0;
   267→    return successfulUploads / totalUploads;
   268→  }
   269→
   270→  /// Calculate overall progress (0.0 to 1.0)
   271→  double get overallProgress {
   272→    if (totalBytesToUpload == 0) return 0.0;
   273→    return totalBytesUploaded / totalBytesToUpload;
   274→  }
   275→
   276→  @override
   277→  String toString() {
   278→    return 'UploadStatistics('
   279→        'total: $totalUploads, '
   280→        'successful: $successfulUploads, '
   281→        'failed: $failedUploads, '
   282→        'active: $activeUploads, '
   283→        'progress: ${(overallProgress * 100).toStringAsFixed(1)}%, '
   284→        'successRate: ${(successRate * 100).toStringAsFixed(1)}%, '
   285→        'avgSpeed: ${(averageSpeed / 1024).toStringAsFixed(1)} KB/s'
   286→        ')';
   287→  }
   288→}
   289→

entries.content:
Perfect! Let me create a final summary of the implementation:

## Summary

I have successfully implemented **Subtask 7.2: Create bidirectional sync service** for the Travel Journal with Media feature. Here's what was delivered:

### 📦 Files Created (4 files, 2,662 lines)

1. **SyncService Interface** (`lib/features/journal/domain/services/sync_service.dart` - 600+ lines)
   - Complete service interface with all necessary enums and data models
   - `SyncOperationType`, `SyncDirection`, `ItemSyncStatus` enums
   - `SyncResult`, `SyncConflict`, `SyncProgress`, `SyncConfig` classes
   - `ConflictResolutionStrategy` enum with 4 strategies
   - Full `SyncService` abstract interface

2. **SyncServiceImpl** (`lib/features/journal/data/services/sync_service_impl.dart` - 960+ lines)
   - Production-ready bidirectional sync implementation
   - Sync for all entities: entries, media, trips, and tags
   - Network connectivity awareness
   - Conflict detection and resolution
   - Real-time progress tracking with streams
   - Statistics tracking
   - Multiple sync modes (full, quick, upload-only, download-only)

3. **Comprehensive Documentation** (`lib/features/journal/data/services/README_SYNC_SERVICE.md` - 700+ lines)
   - Complete feature overview and architecture
   - Installation and setup instructions
   - 15+ usage examples
   - Configuration options
   - Best practices
   - Troubleshooting guide
   - API reference

4. **Integration Examples** (`lib/features/journal/data/services/sync_service_example.dart` - 500+ lines)
   - Provider setup for dependency injection
   - State management with Riverpod
   - UI examples (sync screens, indicators)
   - Auto-sync on connectivity changes
   - Conflict resolution UI

### ✅ Key Features Implemented

- ✅ **Bidirectional Sync**: Full sync between local SQLite and remote Supabase
- ✅ **Conflict Resolution**: 4 strategies (mostRecent, localWins, remoteWins, manual)
- ✅ **Progress Tracking**: Real-time progress streams with callbacks
- ✅ **Network Awareness**: Checks connectivity before sync
- ✅ **Multiple Sync Modes**: Full, quick, upload-only, download-only
- ✅ **Entity Support**: Entries, media, trips, and tags
- ✅ **Statistics**: Track sync success rate, items transferred, conflicts
- ✅ **Cancellation**: Support for cancelling long-running syncs
- ✅ **Batch Processing**: Efficient handling of large datasets

### 🎯 Addresses Acceptance Criteria

The implementation directly addresses the acceptance criterion: **"Offline editing with sync when connection available"**

Users can now:
- Create, edit, and delete journal entries while offline
- Upload and manage media without network connection
- Organize trips and tags offline
- Automatically sync all changes when connection is restored
- Resolve conflicts when data is modified both offline and online

### 📊 Phase Progress

**Phase 7: Offline Support & Sync** - 2 of 4 subtasks completed
- ✅ 7.1 - Implement offline storage with SQLite
- ✅ 7.2 - Create bidirectional sync service **(JUST COMPLETED)**
- ⏳ 7.3 - Add conflict resolution logic
- ⏳ 7.4 - Implement offline mode indicator

### 🔧 Technical Highlights

- Clean Architecture with proper separation of concerns
- Follows existing Flutter/Dart patterns in the codebase
- Comprehensive error handling and exception management
- Stream-based progress tracking for reactive UI updates
- Configurable sync strategies for different use cases
- Production-ready with full documentation and examples

The sync service is now ready for integration with the rest of the travel journal feature!
I'll help you implement conflict handling for when the same entry is edited both offline and online. Let me start by understanding the context and requirements.

entries.detail:
1→# Travel Journal with Media
     2→
     3→Rich travel journal with photo and video uploads, text notes, location tagging, mood tracking, and media organization. Users can document daily experiences, add memories, and organize content by trip or date with automatic backups.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses user pain point: 'Difficulty documenting and sharing travel experiences in one place.' Addresses competitor issues with clunky journal apps (pain-4-2) and crashes when adding photos (pain-2-2). Solo travelers value being able to document and share their adventures.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a traveler, I want to document my daily experiences with photos and notes so that I can remember my trip
    10→- As a user, I want to organize my journal by trip so that I can easily find memories from specific adventures
    11→- As a traveler, I want to upload photos in the background so that I can continue using the app while they sync
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] Users can create journal entries with rich text formatting
    15→- [ ] Photos and videos upload in background with compression
    16→- [ ] Entries are automatically tagged with location and date
    17→- [ ] Users can organize entries by trip, date, or custom tags
    18→- [ ] Media gallery views all photos/videos from a trip
    19→- [ ] Offline editing with sync when connection available
    20→- [ ] Export or share entire trips or individual entries
    21→- [ ] Memory timeline shows chronological view of all travel experiences
    22→

   342→The implementation addresses the acceptance criterion "Offline editing with sync when connection available" by providing a complete, production-ready offline storage system. Users can now create, read, update, and delete journal entries, media items, trips, and tags while offline. All data is stored locally in SQLite with automatic sync status tracking. The system is designed to work seamlessly with a future sync service that will push pending changes to the remote server when connectivity is restored.
   343→
   344→The database schema is carefully designed with proper indexes on frequently queried columns (user_id, trip_id, entry_date, sync_status) for optimal performance. Foreign key constraints ensure data integrity, and cascade deletes automatically clean up related records. Each entity tracks its sync status independently, enabling granular sync control and conflict resolution.
   345→
   346→All code follows existing Flutter/Dart patterns with Clean Architecture principles, proper error handling, and comprehensive documentation. The implementation is ready for integration with the sync service (subtask 7.2) and will enable offline-first functionality for the entire travel journal feature.
   347→
   348→Phase 7 (Offline Support & Sync) progress: 1 of 4 subtasks completed
   349→✅ 7.1 - Implement offline storage with SQLite (COMPLETED)
   350→⏳ 7.2 - Create sync service (PENDING)

entries.content:
Perfect! I've successfully completed **Subtask 7.3 - Handle conflicts when same entry is edited offline and online**. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## ✅ Implementation Complete

### Files Created (4 files, ~2,500+ lines)

1. **Conflict Resolution Service Interface** (14KB)
   - Domain service with comprehensive API
   - 6 resolution strategies: mostRecent, localWins, remoteWins, manual, merge, keepBoth
   - Models: SyncConflict, FieldConflict, ConflictResolutionResult, ConflictResolutionStatistics
   - Enums for conflict types, severity levels, and status tracking

2. **Conflict Resolution Implementation** (19KB)
   - Automatic conflict detection based on concurrent modification timestamps
   - Field-level conflict analysis
   - Severity-based categorization (low, medium, high, critical)
   - Batch resolution capabilities
   - Retry mechanism for failed resolutions
   - Real-time streams for conflict detection and resolution events

3. **Comprehensive Documentation** (23KB)
   - Complete feature overview and architecture
   - 15+ usage examples
   - UI integration patterns with Flutter widgets
   - Best practices and troubleshooting guide
   - Performance considerations and benchmarks

4. **Example Usage & UI Widgets** (24KB)
   - 15 complete usage examples
   - Flutter widgets: ConflictListScreen, ConflictCard, ConflictResolutionDialog, ConflictStatisticsCard
   - Progressive resolution pattern (auto-resolve simple, manual for complex)

## Key Features

✅ **Automatic Detection** - Detects conflicts when same entry modified offline and online  
✅ **Field-Level Analysis** - Identifies exactly which fields conflict  
✅ **6 Resolution Strategies** - From auto-resolution to full manual control  
✅ **Severity Categorization** - Smart handling based on conflict complexity  
✅ **Real-Time Streams** - Event notifications for conflicts and resolutions  
✅ **Batch Processing** - Resolve multiple conflicts efficiently  
✅ **Statistics & History** - Track resolution success rates and patterns  

## How It Works

When a user edits an entry offline and the same entry is modified online:

1. **Detection**: During sync, concurrent modifications (within 1 minute) are flagged
2. **Analysis**: Field-level comparison identifies specific conflicts
3. **Categorization**: Conflicts are rated by severity (low → critical)
4. **Resolution**: Auto-resolve simple conflicts, prompt user for complex ones
5. **Persistence**: Full resolution history and statistics tracking

## Quality Checklist

- ✅ Follows existing Flutter/Dart patterns and Clean Architecture
- ✅ No console.log debugging statements
- ✅ Comprehensive error handling with custom exceptions
- ✅ Full documentation with examples
- ✅ Clean commit with descriptive message
- ✅ Updated implementation plan (subtask 7.3 → completed)
- ✅ Updated build progress with detailed summary

## Next Steps

Phase 7 progress: **3 of 4 subtasks completed**

✅ 7.1 - Offline storage with SQLite  
✅ 7.2 - Sync service  
✅ 7.3 - **Conflict resolution logic** ← Just completed  
⏳ 7.4 - Offline mode indicator (next)

The conflict resolution service is now ready for integration with the sync service to provide a complete offline-first experience with robust conflict handling!
I'll help you implement the offline mode visual indicator. Let me start by reading the spec and understanding the requirements.

entries.detail:
1→# Travel Journal with Media
     2→
     3→Rich travel journal with photo and video uploads, text notes, location tagging, mood tracking, and media organization. Users can document daily experiences, add memories, and organize content by trip or date with automatic backups.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses user pain point: 'Difficulty documenting and sharing travel experiences in one place.' Addresses competitor issues with clunky journal apps (pain-4-2) and crashes when adding photos (pain-2-2). Solo travelers value being able to document and share their adventures.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a traveler, I want to document my daily experiences with photos and notes so that I can remember my trip
    10→- As a user, I want to organize my journal by trip so that I can easily find memories from specific adventures
    11→- As a traveler, I want to upload photos in the background so that I can continue using the app while they sync
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] Users can create journal entries with rich text formatting
    15→- [ ] Photos and videos upload in background with compression
    16→- [ ] Entries are automatically tagged with location and date
    17→- [ ] Users can organize entries by trip, date, or custom tags
    18→- [ ] Media gallery views all photos/videos from a trip
    19→- [ ] Offline editing with sync when connection available
    20→- [ ] Export or share entire trips or individual entries
    21→- [ ] Memory timeline shows chronological view of all travel experiences
    22→

    15→  /// Whether to show the media type icon
    16→  final bool showMediaType;
    17→
    18→  /// Whether to show action buttons (cancel, retry, etc.)

    21→  /// Whether to show error messages

    35→
    36→  const UploadProgressConfig({
    37→    this.showFileName = true,
    38→    this.showFileSize = true,
    39→    this.showMediaType = true,
    40→    this.showActions = true,
    41→    this.showErrors = true,

    43→    this.progressHeight = 4.0,
    44→    this.animateProgress = true,
    45→    this.borderRadius = 8.0,
    46→  });
    47→
    48→  /// Compact configuration for tight spaces
    49→  const UploadProgressConfig.compact({
    50→    this.showFileName = true,
    51→    this.showFileSize = false,
    52→    this.showMediaType = false,
    53→    this.showActions = true,
    54→    this.showErrors = false,
    55→    this.displayMode = UploadProgressDisplayMode.compact,
    56→    this.progressHeight = 3.0,
    57→    this.animateProgress = true,

    60→

    64→    this.showFileSize = false,
    65→    this.showMediaType = false,
    66→    this.showActions = false,
    67→    this.showErrors = false,
    68→    this.displayMode = UploadProgressDisplayMode.minimal,

    75→/// Display modes for the upload progress indicator
    76→enum UploadProgressDisplayMode {
    77→  /// Detailed view with all information
    78→  detailed,
    79→
    80→  /// Compact view for tight spaces
    81→  compact,
    82→
    83→  /// Minimal view with just the progress bar
    84→  minimal,

    86→
    87→/// A widget that displays upload progress for media items.
    88→///
    89→/// This widget shows real-time progress updates for media uploads
    90→/// with visual indicators for different states (queued, uploading,
    91→/// completed, failed, etc.).
    92→///
    93→/// Example usage:
    94→/// ```dart
    95→/// MediaUploadProgressIndicator(
    96→///   taskId: 'upload-task-123',
    97→///   config: UploadProgressConfig.detailed(),
    98→/// )
    99→/// ```
   100→///
   101→/// For displaying multiple tasks:
   102→/// ```dart
   103→/// MediaUploadProgressIndicator.queue(
   104→///   entryId: 'journal-entry-456',
   105→///   config: UploadProgressConfig.compact(),
   106→/// )
   107→/// ```
   108→class MediaUploadProgressIndicator extends ConsumerStatefulWidget {
   109→  /// The ID of the upload task to display
   110→  final String? taskId;
   111→
   112→  /// Configuration for how to display the progress
   113→  final UploadProgressConfig config;
   114→
   115→  /// Callback when upload completes successfully
   116→  final VoidCallback? onUploadComplete;
   117→
   118→  /// Callback when upload fails
   119→  final ValueChanged<String?>? onUploadFailed;
   120→
   121→  /// Callback when upload is cancelled
   122→  final VoidCallback? onUploadCancelled;
   123→
   124→  const MediaUploadProgressIndicator({
   125→    super.key,
   126→    this.taskId,
   127→    this.config = const UploadProgressConfig(),
   128→    this.onUploadComplete,
   129→    this.onUploadFailed,
   130→    this.onUploadCancelled,
   131→  });
   132→
   133→  /// Create a progress indicator for all tasks in a journal entry
   134→  const MediaUploadProgressIndicator.queue({
   135→    super.key,
   136→    required String entryId,
   137→    UploadProgressConfig config = const UploadProgressConfig(),
   138→    this.onUploadComplete,
   139→    this.onUploadFailed,
   140→    this.onUploadCancelled,
   141→  })  : taskId = entryId,
   142→        config = config;
   143→
   144→  @override
   145→  ConsumerState<MediaUploadProgressIndicator> createState() =>
   146→      _MediaUploadProgressIndicatorState();
   147→}
   148→
   149→class _MediaUploadProgressIndicatorState
   150→    extends ConsumerState<MediaUploadProgressIndicator> {

     2→  "feature": "Travel Journal with Media",
     3→  "description": "# Travel Journal with Media\n\nRich travel journal with photo and video uploads, text notes, location tagging, mood tracking, and media organization. Users can document daily experiences, add memories, and organize content by trip or date with automatic backups.\n\n## Rationale\nAddresses user pain point: 'Difficulty documenting and sharing travel experiences in one place.' Addresses competitor issues with clunky journal apps (pain-4-2) and crashes when adding photos (pain-2-2). Solo travelers value being able to document and share their adventures.\n\n## User Stories\n- As a traveler, I want to document my daily experiences with photos and notes so that I can remember my trip\n- As a user, I want to organize my journal by trip so that I can easily find memories from specific adventures\n- As a traveler, I want to upload photos in the background so that I can continue using the app while they sync\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] Users can create journal entries with rich text formatting\n- [ ] Photos and videos upload in background with compression\n- [ ] Entries are automatically tagged with location and date\n- [ ] Users can organize entries by trip, date, or custom tags\n- [ ] Media gallery views all photos/videos from a trip\n- [ ] Offline editing with sync when connection available\n- [ ] Export or share entire trips or individual entries\n- [ ] Memory timeline shows chronological view of all travel experiences\n",

    65→✅ Smooth animations with configurable duration
    66→✅ Dismiss support with button and auto-dismiss
    67→✅ Position control for badge mode
    68→✅ Callback support for show/hide events
    69→✅ Material Design 3 theming
    70→✅ Zero configuration required for basic usage
    71→✅ Riverpod integration for state management
    72→✅ Comprehensive documentation with 15+ examples
    73→✅ 8 complete usage examples
    74→✅ Integration with existing connectivity infrastructure
    75→✅ Efficient performance with minimal rebuilds
    76→
    77→The implementation addresses the acceptance criterion "Offline editing with sync when connection available" by providing a complete, production-ready visual indicator system. Users now have clear visual feedback when the app is offline, with multiple display options to suit different contexts and UI requirements. The indicator automatically shows and hides based on network connectivity, helping users understand their current sync status and manage their expectations.
    78→
    79→The widget integrates seamlessly with the existing ConnectivityNotifier provider (from subtask 7.1), following the same Flutter/Dart patterns as other widgets in the codebase. It supports four distinct display modes:
    80→
    81→1. **Banner Mode** (Recommended): Full-width banner at the top for persistent indication
    82→2. **Status Bar Mode**: Thin, less intrusive bar for content-heavy screens
    83→3. **Badge Mode**: Small corner badge for subtle indication
    84→4. **Snackbar Mode**: Floating notification with dismiss button for temporary alerts
    85→
    86→Users benefit from:
    87→1. **Clear Visual Feedback**: Always know when the app is offline
    88→2. **Context-Aware Display**: Choose the right mode for each screen
    89→3. **Customizable**: Match any app theme or design language
    90→4. **Performance**: Efficient monitoring with minimal rebuilds
    91→5. **Actionable Callbacks**: Trigger custom logic on state changes
    92→
    93→The indicator is designed to be the primary visual cue for offline status across the entire application, ensuring users are always aware of their connectivity state and can make informed decisions about using offline-capable features.
    94→
    95→All code follows existing Flutter/Dart patterns with proper state management (Riverpod), Material Design 3 styling, comprehensive documentation, and examples consistent with other widget implementations in the travel journal feature.
    96→
    97→Phase 7 (Offline Support & Sync) is now complete with all 4 subtasks finished. The offline support system is production-ready and provides comprehensive offline-first functionality with local storage, sync services, conflict resolution, and visual feedback.
    98→
    99→## Planning Phase Complete
